creator: hsw@acm.org
text:
My mistake, the Makefile looks ok.
I think I was just confused as the port I was compiling wanted mm.10
and the mm ports was already up to mm.11.

I will be compiling the same ports again on another machine some time
next week and will try to keep a more careful note of any errors.

Please close this PR as I do not now think it relates to the problem I had.

Thanks.
  Chris Hall.

In message <20000518234751.B14072@norn.ca.eu.org>,
  Chris Piazza writes:
>On Tue, May 16, 2000 at 01:46:47PM +0800, hsw@acm.org wrote:
>>
>> 	When installing devel/mm as a dependancy
>> 	the mm.so file is not visible in ldconfig -r
>>
>> >How-To-Repeat:
>>
>> 	Install a port dependant on devel/mm
>>
>...
>> post-install:
>> 	${LDCONFIG} -m ${PREFIX}/lib
>>
>
>It seems to work here...
>
>(right after install)
>norn[~]% ldconfig -r | grep mm
>        170:-lmm.11 => /usr/local/lib/libmm.so.11
>
>The port has had the equivalent of these lines since it was imported...
>
>post-install:
>        @${SETENV} OBJFORMAT=${PORTOBJFORMAT} ${LDCONFIG} -m ${PREFIX}/lib
>
>Can you try again and see if it works?
>
>-Chris
>--
>cpiazza@jaxon.net      | yawn.....
>cpiazza@FreeBSD.org    | Abbotsford, BC, Canada
>

---
Christopher Hall <c.hall@computer.org>
